summary: Full backend + frontend test of Tuncel Textile e-commerce MVP. 12/13 backend tests pass. All frontend flows work end-to-end (home, shop filters, product detail, cart CRUD, Stripe redirect, about, mobile menu).

critical.issue: Returns 500. Error from emergentintegrations: `CheckoutError: Failed to retrieve session status: No such checkout.session: cs_test_...`. The session_id was just successfully created by POST /api/checkout/session a moment earlier but the subsequent status lookup cannot find it on Stripe. Frontend /checkout/success page polls this endpoint, so the post-payment UX is broken. Likely an emergentintegrations library/Stripe test-key issue where create and retrieve hit different accounts/modes. Needs main-agent investigation (possibly web_search for emergentintegrations stripe status bug, or check stripe key/mode used by StripeCheckout.get_checkout_status).

minor.issue: Logs `Webhook handling failed: Unexpected error processing webhook: 'type'` on invalid payloads. Endpoint correctly returns 400 but log noise suggests library accesses body['type'] without validation. Not functionally broken.

action_items: Investigate GET /api/checkout/status/{session_id} 500 error — Stripe reports `No such checkout.session` immediately after creation. Check if StripeCheckout(api_key=...) in the status handler is using the same key/mode as creation, or if emergentintegrations needs a version bump. Consider a retry/backoff or a more graceful fallback that returns existing transaction data without polling Stripe when unavailable.
Optional: improve webhook handler to gracefully log when Stripe-Signature is missing/invalid instead of triggering KeyError 'type'.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is a single 310-line file — acceptable for MVP but consider splitting into routers/models/seed modules as it grows beyond 700 lines.
MongoDB _id is correctly excluded via projection ({'_id': 0}) everywhere — good.
Checkout total is computed server-side from DB prices (not trusting frontend) — correct security practice.
payment_transactions status guard `$nin: ['paid', 'failed', 'expired']` is good to prevent regression of finalized states.
emergentintegrations error in /checkout/status is not caught — wrap in try/except to return 502 or cached DB status instead of raw 500.
test_credentials: N/A — no auth in this app. Stripe test key set in /app/backend/.env (sk_test_emergent).
seed_data_creation: None. 11 products auto-seeded by backend startup handler verified via API.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest file at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py has 13 tests; only TestCheckout::test_checkout_status fails due to Stripe lookup issue. Frontend testids confirmed working: home-page, shop-page-{men|women|accessories}, shop-filter-{all|hoodie|tshirt}, product-card-*, product-size-*, product-color-*, add-to-cart-button, buy-now-button, nav-cart-count, cart-item-*, cart-qty-increase-*, cart-qty-decrease-*, cart-remove-*, cart-subtotal, checkout-button, nav-mobile-menu-trigger, mobile-nav-link-*, about-page. Stripe checkout redirect confirmed (checkout.stripe.com URL reached after clicking checkout-button).
rca of the issue: POST /api/checkout/session succeeds and returns a valid cs_test_* session_id plus checkout.stripe.com URL. Immediately calling GET /api/checkout/status/{session_id} with that same id fails: emergentintegrations raises `CheckoutError: No such checkout.session`. Reproduction: run `pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py::TestCheckout -v`. Mitigation options: (a) wrap stripe_checkout.get_checkout_status in try/except and fall back to the locally stored payment_transactions record (b) verify STRIPE_API_KEY is usable for retrieval not just creation (c) web_search for emergentintegrations stripe get_checkout_status known issue. Currently the /checkout/success polling page will fail to show paid status even after a successful real payment.
